title: Tarek Jaziri-Arjona
text:
Hi! I’m a Ph.D. candidate at the EUI, where I focus on political behavior, social networks and new parties. I’ve had the privilege of visiting institutions like NYU and the LSE. I hold an M.A. in Social Sciences from the IC3JM and a B.A. in Sociology & Political Science from the same university.
My research is driven by an interest in political behavior, social norms, networks and political parties. I am currently working on the emergence of new parties, the spread of social norms, the gender gap in voting for green parties and the electoral consequences of policies, such as the enfranchisement of immigrants or water restrictions during a drought.

title: Research
text:
Publications

Jaziri-Arjona, T. (2021). Las brechas electorales generacional y urbano-rural en el voto a Vox. In Oliván Navarro, F. (Eds.), El toro por los cuernos: Vox, la extrema derecha europea y el voto obrero. Tecnos.



Working Projects

Social Networks and the Educational Gap between Early and Late New Party Voters
Mobility, Reference Group Change, and the Spread of Political Norms: How Summer Holidays normalize the Radical Right in Spain (with Vicente Valentim)
Clusters of Radical Politics? Heterogeneous Social Networks as a Safeguard against the Radical Right (with Jona De Jong)
Local Female Political Representation and the Gender Gap in Voting for the Greens (with Ingvild Zinober)
No rain in sight - The electoral effect of water restrictions in France (with Nina Lopez-Uroz & Rens Chazottes)
The Electoral Consequences of Immigrants Enfranchasiment (with Simon Hix)
